<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] The algorithmic zone ID 'e2e' was assigned because the e2e test files dominate by count; ensure CI tooling and zone-based filtering do not inadvertently exclude validate.ts from production code analysis passes.
[observation] [info] Zero external coupling means the schema validator can be evolved or replaced without impacting any other zone at runtime — a desirable property worth preserving as schema versions are added.
[observation] [info] src/schema/validate.ts is the sole production file in this zone alongside three test files spanning all test levels — this is a well-tested, isolated module and a positive example of the single-responsibility principle.
[pattern] [warning] src/schema/index.ts and src/schema/v1.ts function as a shared foundation layer (imported by 3–4 zones, zero outgoing imports) but are classified under a zone named 'schema-validation' dominated by test files — rename or restructure to reflect the foundation role and prevent the schema types from being treated as validation-layer internals.
[anti-pattern] [warning] tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts are CLI behavior tests misclassified into the schema-validation zone because they happen to import schema types; this makes the schema zone appear to own CLI e2e coverage and splits CLI test files across two zones — move both files to the CLI e2e test zone.
[suggestion] [warning] Create a dedicated cli-e2e zone and move tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts into it — without a canonical CLI e2e zone, zone-level test coverage reports cannot surface CLI end-to-end test presence, and new CLI e2e tests have no clear zone home.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling (0.0) confirm that schema validation has no runtime dependencies on the rest of the codebase — an ideal property for a validation layer.
- Co-locating e2e, integration, and unit tests with the single production file means every layer of the test pyramid is represented for this module.
- The zone name 'e2e' is a clustering artifact; the canonical architectural identity is schema validation, which has a broader test scope than the label implies.
- src/schema/validate.ts is the sole production file in this zone alongside three test files spanning all test levels — this is a well-tested, isolated module and a positive example of the single-responsibility principle.
- The algorithmic zone ID 'e2e' was assigned because the e2e test files dominate by count; ensure CI tooling and zone-based filtering do not inadvertently exclude validate.ts from production code analysis passes.
- Zero external coupling means the schema validator can be evolved or replaced without impacting any other zone at runtime — a desirable property worth preserving as schema versions are added.
- The schema-validation zone's perfect isolation (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0) combined with src/schema/index.ts and src/schema/v1.ts being imported by 3–4 other zones reveals a structural mismatch — the production schema modules are load-bearing cross-zone infrastructure but are classified in a zone named for validation; this misleads zone-based tooling about where schema types live architecturally.
- The schema zone is effectively a hidden foundation layer: other zones depend on it at runtime but it has no outgoing runtime dependencies, which is the correct property for a foundation — however it should be explicitly named and governed as such rather than discovered as a side-effect of test co-location.
- src/schema/index.ts and src/schema/v1.ts function as a shared foundation layer (imported by 3–4 zones, zero outgoing imports) but are classified under a zone named 'schema-validation' dominated by test files — rename or restructure to reflect the foundation role and prevent the schema types from being treated as validation-layer internals.
- tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts exercise CLI command behavior end-to-end, not schema validation logic; they were co-located here because they import schema types, but their architectural home is the CLI e2e test zone — their presence in schema-validation inflates this zone's apparent test coverage of the schema module and obscures the true scope of CLI e2e testing.
- tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts are CLI behavior tests misclassified into the schema-validation zone because they happen to import schema types; this makes the schema zone appear to own CLI e2e coverage and splits CLI test files across two zones — move both files to the CLI e2e test zone.
- The zone contains exactly one production file (src/schema/validate.ts) and four test files spanning three test levels; the zone name 'schema-validation' accurately describes only the production file, but the presence of two CLI e2e test files means the zone's centroid in the import graph is pulled toward CLI testing, not schema validation — this naming drift will worsen as more CLI e2e tests are added.
- tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts are the only e2e test files visible in this zone's file list; if there is no dedicated CLI e2e zone, these files represent the entirety of CLI e2e coverage — their misclassification into schema-validation means CLI e2e coverage is invisible in any zone-level test coverage report.
- Create a dedicated cli-e2e zone and move tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts into it — without a canonical CLI e2e zone, zone-level test coverage reports cannot surface CLI end-to-end test presence, and new CLI e2e tests have no clear zone home.
- [call graph] 87 internal calls, 10 outgoing, 11 incoming (cohesion: 0.9, coupling: 0.1)

</insights>
